page: Amazon Web Services m6in.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 54 MB L3 cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. Memory capacity stands at 192.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark evaluations, the m6in.12xlarge demonstrates strong capabilities in specific compute and network-related tasks. It achieves a Passmark CPU score of 48435.76 and a Passmark CPU encryption test score of 43448.8, both placing in the top 25% tier. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 41.66 GB/sec. For cryptographic and vector operations, the processor supports AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and SHA_NI instruction sets. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 2503.58 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write bandwidths reach 158.1 GB/sec and 69.22 GB/sec, respectively.

From a resource density perspective, the m6in.12xlarge offers a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio combined with high network performance, featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. This makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that require high network throughput without the overhead of local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators. The instance is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, enterprise application hosting, database operations, and moderate-scale language model inference, where its strong cryptographic and image processing capabilities can be fully utilized.
description: Amazon Web Services m6in.12xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance optimized for network and EBS performance. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) from an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate strong performance in CPU-bound tasks, encryption, and static web serving, with a Passmark CPU score of 48435.76 and web throughput of 41.66 GB/sec. It is highly suitable for network-intensive workloads, enterprise applications, and web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for network-heavy enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A network-optimized general-purpose instance featuring high-bandwidth connectivity and balanced Intel Xeon compute resources for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
192.0 GB of DDR4 RAM providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
High-speed baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps for data-intensive transfers
Strong performance in encryption, image processing, and static web serving
Optimized for network-heavy enterprise applications and web serving without local storage needs
